response.justification: Candidate has only ~6 months of C++ exposure from over a decade ago at National Instruments (Dec 2014 – Jun 2015), with no recent C++ work, no Windows-platform focus, no SQL Server/Oracle depth, and no fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience. The bulk of their career is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS systems — strong engineer profile but a poor match for a C++/fixed-income consulting role.
strengths: Senior-level engineering experience with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s)
Some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on an engineering IDE
concerns: No recent or substantial C++ experience; only ~6 months a decade ago
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain background
No demonstrated SQL Server/Oracle depth or Windows-platform development focus

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C++ on Windows
No fixed-income or capital-markets domain experience
